functions.name: signal.connect
functions.description: Connect a bound PySide6 signal to a Python callable with an explicit connection type when required.
functions.longDescription:
Connect a bound PySide6 signal to a Python callable with an explicit connection type when required.

Historical workflow absorbed from `connect signal to slot`:
```python
button.clicked.connect(self.on_scan_clicked)
```

Historical workflow absorbed from `connect signal to lambda`:
```python
button.clicked.connect(lambda: self.start_scan(timeout_ms=2000))
```

Historical workflow absorbed from `queued cross-thread signal`:
```python
from PySide6.QtCore import Qt

worker.measurement_ready.connect(
    self.update_display,
    Qt.ConnectionType.QueuedConnection,
)
```

Historical workflow absorbed from `unique connection flag`:
```python
from PySide6.QtCore import Qt

button.clicked.connect(
    self.on_scan_clicked,
    Qt.ConnectionType.UniqueConnection,
)
```

options.label: Qt.ConnectionType.AutoConnection
options.description: Let Qt choose direct or queued delivery according to the sender and receiver thread affinity.

options.label: Qt.ConnectionType.DirectConnection
options.description: Invoke the slot immediately in the emitting thread.

options.label: Qt.ConnectionType.QueuedConnection
options.description: Queue delivery to the receiver event loop and return immediately from the emitter.

options.label: Qt.ConnectionType.BlockingQueuedConnection
options.description: Queue delivery and block the emitting thread until the receiver finishes. Do not use this between objects in the same thread.

options.label: Qt.ConnectionType.UniqueConnection
options.description: Reject creation of an additional equivalent connection.

options.label: Qt.ConnectionType.SingleShotConnection
options.description: Automatically disconnect the connection after the first successful delivery.

functions.name: QTimer
functions.description: Construct a high-level Qt timer associated with an event loop.
functions.longDescription:
Construct a high-level Qt timer associated with an event loop.

Historical workflow absorbed from `QTimer periodic callback`:
```python
from PySide6.QtCore import QTimer

self.timer = QTimer(self)
self.timer.setInterval(250)
self.timer.timeout.connect(self.poll_device)
self.timer.start()
```

Historical workflow absorbed from `QTimer reusable single-shot timer`:
```python
from PySide6.QtCore import QTimer

self.retry_timer = QTimer(self)
self.retry_timer.setSingleShot(True)
self.retry_timer.setInterval(750)
self.retry_timer.timeout.connect(self.retry_connection)
self.retry_timer.start()
```

functions.longDescription: Connect the QTimer timeout signal to a callback.

functions.name: timer.start
functions.description: Start or restart a QTimer with a millisecond interval.
functions.longDescription:
Start or restart a QTimer with a millisecond interval.

Historical workflow absorbed from `QTimer start stop interval and active state`:
```python
self.timer.setInterval(1000)
self.timer.start()

if self.timer.isActive():
    print("remaining ms:", self.timer.remainingTime())

self.timer.stop()
self.timer.start(250)
```

Historical workflow absorbed from `QTimer zero-delay incremental work`:
```python
from PySide6.QtCore import QTimer

self.worker_timer = QTimer(self)
self.worker_timer.timeout.connect(self.process_one_item)
self.worker_timer.start(0)
```

functions.name: QProgressDialog modal loop
functions.signature:
from PySide6.QtCore import Qt
from PySide6.QtWidgets import QProgressDialog

progress = QProgressDialog("Exporting reports...", "Cancel", 0, len(files), self)
progress.setWindowModality(Qt.WindowModality.WindowModal)

for index, file_name in enumerate(files):
    progress.setValue(index)
    if progress.wasCanceled():
        break
    self.export_one_report(file_name)

progress.setValue(len(files))

functions.name: serial_port.readAll
functions.description: Read all currently buffered serial bytes.
functions.longDescription:
Read all currently buffered serial bytes.

Historical workflow absorbed from `QSerialPort readyRead buffer`:
```python
self.serial.readyRead.connect(self.on_serial_ready_read)
self._rx_buffer = bytearray()

def on_serial_ready_read(self) -> None:
    self._rx_buffer.extend(bytes(self.serial.readAll()))
    while b"\n" in self._rx_buffer:
        line, _, self._rx_buffer = self._rx_buffer.partition(b"\n")
        self.handle_line(line.decode("utf-8", errors="replace"))
```

Historical workflow absorbed from `QNetworkReply error and cleanup`:
```python
from PySide6.QtNetwork import QNetworkReply

def on_http_finished(self, reply: QNetworkReply) -> None:
    try:
        if reply.error() != QNetworkReply.NetworkError.NoError:
            raise RuntimeError(reply.errorString())
        payload = bytes(reply.readAll())
        self.handle_payload(payload)
    finally:
        reply.deleteLater()
```

Construct a Qt thread controller. Prefer moving a QObject worker to the thread for ordinary application logic.

Historical workflow absorbed from `QObject worker moved to QThread`:
```python
from PySide6.QtCore import QObject, QThread, Signal, Slot

class Worker(QObject):
    finished = Signal()
    result_ready = Signal(object)

    @Slot()
    def run(self) -> None:
        result = perform_scan()
        self.result_ready.emit(result)
        self.finished.emit()

self.thread = QThread(self)
self.worker = Worker()
self.worker.moveToThread(self.thread)
self.thread.started.connect(self.worker.run)
self.worker.result_ready.connect(self.update_display)
self.worker.finished.connect(self.thread.quit)
self.worker.finished.connect(self.worker.deleteLater)
self.thread.finished.connect(self.thread.deleteLater)
self.thread.start()
```

functions.name: QQmlApplicationEngine
functions.description: Construct a QML application engine.
functions.longDescription:
Construct a QML application engine.

Historical workflow absorbed from `QQmlApplicationEngine load QML`:
```python
import sys
from PySide6.QtCore import QUrl
from PySide6.QtGui import QGuiApplication
from PySide6.QtQml import QQmlApplicationEngine

app = QGuiApplication(sys.argv)
engine = QQmlApplicationEngine()
engine.load(QUrl("qrc:/Main.qml"))
if not engine.rootObjects():
    raise RuntimeError("Unable to load QML")
sys.exit(app.exec())
```
